entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: General term for the organisms being studied in the context of stress responses

entities.name: STRESS FACTORS
entities.description: Various factors that induce stress in plants

entities.name: EPIGENETICS
entities.description: The study of heritable changes in gene expression that do not involve changes to the DNA sequence

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by living organisms such as pests or pathogens

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by non-living environmental factors such as drought or salinity

entities.name: PLANT ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of plants to adjust to their environment to survive and reproduce

entities.name: PLANT MOLECULAR BIOLOGY
entities.description: The field of biology focusing on the molecular basis of plant life

entities.name: MOLECULAR MECHANISMS
entities.description: The specific molecular processes that control plant interactions and responses

entities.name: PLANT RESPONSES
entities.description: The physiological and molecular reactions of plants to stress

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing crop traits for better yield and resilience

entities.name: EPIGENETIC MODIFICATIONS
entities.description: Chemical changes to DNA or histones that regulate gene expression without altering the sequence

entities.name: PROFILING AND ENGINEERING
entities.description: Modern technologies used to analyze and modify plants at a genome-wide scale

entities.name: STRESS CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental states that trigger stress responses in plants

entities.name: EPIGENETIC REGULATION
entities.description: The process by which epigenetic modifications control plant stress responses

entities.name: DETECTION METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used to identify genome-wide epigenetic modifications

entities.name: PLANT PHENOTYPES
entities.description: The observable physical or biochemical characteristics of a plant

entities.name: STRESS MEMORY
entities.description: The ability of plants to retain a memory of previous stress to respond more effectively to future stress

entities.name: BREEDING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Planned approaches to cross plants to improve specific traits

entities.name: CROP PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The overall productivity and health of a crop under various conditions
